summary.current_state: Both adapters are stable-supported for their documented surfaces.
summary.goal: Close or explicitly exclude remaining production-certification boundaries so strict-final status can pass.

gates.next_action: Keep the Glue/Iceberg benchmark in release validation and rerun after hash-diff runtime or packaging changes.
gates.acceptance: Athena audit rows, Glue run ids, Iceberg snapshot metrics, cost rows and redacted failure evidence prove bounded behavior.

gates.next_action: Keep the LF consumer matrix in release validation and rerun after Lake Formation, IAM or Glue runtime changes. Treat arbitrary row-filter and mask expressions as workload-specific review items.
gates.acceptance: Allowed principals read exactly declared rows/columns and denied principals cannot exceed the contract; arbitrary unreviewed expressions are outside strict-final.

gates.next_action: Keep MSK Serverless in release validation. Treat Confluent-compatible Kafka as an optional compatibility item, not an AWS maturity blocker.
gates.acceptance: MSK Serverless checkpoint, offset, failure, stream and cost evidence semantics are recorded.

gates.next_action: Keep historical and snapshot soft-delete outside the AWS stable-final claim until Databricks/AWS parity contracts are implemented and certified.
gates.acceptance: Status report and criteria either exclude the behavior or link E2E parity evidence.

gates.next_action: Keep smoke-task-graph in the release validation set and rerun after task/runtime deployment changes.
gates.acceptance: Task graph deploys, root task executes, dependent task succeeds, task history reaches terminal success and cleanup removes smoke artifacts.

gates.next_action: Keep the Snowflake benchmark in release validation and rerun after hash-diff runtime, procedure or packaging changes.
gates.acceptance: Snowflake query ids, query-history cost signals, hash-diff candidate metrics and control-table rows prove bounded behavior.

gates.next_action: Keep row access and masking policy certification outside the Snowflake stable-final claim for accounts where ROW ACCESS POLICY is unavailable.
gates.acceptance: Access-policy smoke result and status reports explicitly exclude unavailable policy features without weakening supported table grants, comments/tags or validate-only governance.

gates.next_action: Keep Snowpipe, Streams, Snowpipe Streaming and Kafka connector ingestion outside the Snowflake stable-final claim until a connector/runtime mapping is implemented and certified.
gates.acceptance: Decision manifest, planner/status output and docs explicitly exclude continuous ingestion from stable-final without silent fallback.

gates.next_action: Keep historical and snapshot soft-delete outside the Snowflake stable-final claim until Databricks/Snowflake parity contracts are implemented and certified.
gates.acceptance: Status report and criteria either exclude the behavior or link E2E parity evidence.
